source_intake_record.raw_question_text:
Aladdin's carpet has the shape of a square. Along each edge there are two rows of dots (see diagram). The number of points is the same along each edge. How many dots in total does the carpet have?
<image1>

image_dependency: The total number of dots cannot be determined from text alone because the per-edge dot arrangement/count must be read from the image
multi_step: Likely requires first reading/counting the dot pattern from the diagram, then computing the total
The square-with-dots setup suggests handling repeated edge structure and possible corner/overlap counting rather than a single direct lookup
Answer is numeric with no choices, so the solver likely must derive the count from the visual arrangement
verifiability: The result can be checked by recounting dots from the diagram or by validating an edge-counting formula

decision_reasons: question, image, and numeric answer are all present, so the core semantics are recoverable and the sample should remain in the candidate pool
image_dependency_score and verifiability_score are high, indicating the item is grounded in the diagram and the answer can be checked
risk_flags includes 'small_visual_counting_task', which lowers priority relative to richer reasoning items
multi_step_score is only moderate, so despite complete assets this is better treated as a lower-priority candidate than a top-priority keep
